title: ChannelConfig
description: Contents of <workspace>/channels.config.json. Human-editable: named remote entry points that map to the same run/status/approve/reject actions the web UI and CLI already call. Never store a secret in this file — `tokenEnvVar` names an environment variable the server reads at request time.

id.description: Unique within this file. Path segment: POST /api/channels/{id}/actions.

kind.description: Transport adapter. Only a bare authenticated-HTTP channel ships today; future adapters (slack, discord, email, ...) get their own `kind` and translate their native message shape into the same action call — they don't need a new action surface.

tokenEnvVar.description: Name of the environment variable holding this channel's bearer token. The token value itself must never be written to this file.

allowedActions.description: Scopes what this channel may do. A read-only status bot should list just ["status"].
